§6Wood Oil§f

§aRenewable liquid from wood processing — crack it, burn it, or turn it into biofuel!§f

Wood Oil is a viscous, flammable, polluting liquid providing §e110.0 kTU§f per bucket. It is produced by processing wood products in the §bCombination Oven§f, making it one of the few fully renewable petrochemical feedstocks. Wood Oil does not require crude oil at all — it is produced entirely from trees.

§6Production§f (Combination Oven):
§b• Sawdust Briquette§f → Charcoal + §e500mB§f Wood Oil.
§b• Oak Wood§f → Charcoal + §e250mB§f Wood Oil.
§b• Spruce Sapling§f → Wood Ash + §e50mB§f Wood Oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: §e100mB§f + §e200mB§f Steam → §e40mB§f Heating Oil + §e10mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + Low-Pressure Steam.
§b• Industrial Mixer§f: §e500mB§f Wood Oil + §e500mB§f Fish Oil / Sunflower Seed Oil → §e1,000mB§f Biofuel.
§b• Industrial Solidification Machine§f: §e1,000mB§f → §e1×§f Wood Tar.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e3,000mB§f → Charcoal + §e300mB§f Coker Gas.
§b• Ore Acidizer§f: With various material powders to produce chemical dyes.

§eTip§f: Wood Oil is an excellent renewable resource for §bbiofuel§f production and early polymer/tar crafting. A single stack of wood processed through the Combination Oven yields hundreds of buckets. Combine with the §bStirling Sawmill§f for maximum log-to-oil efficiency!
